# Questions in Livestream 207

Live Stream #207: Root of the Problem

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=695" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">11:35</a> | Did you really set up an affiliate link to your own book? | Yes. He said it does not raise the buyer's price and helps recover margin that would otherwise go to Amazon. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=1022" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:02</a> | Have you tried getting your books into bookstores? | He said that is very difficult without big-publisher distribution and access to the locked-up retail channel. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=1169" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">19:29</a> | How much is the ebook version? | He said it is $5.50 and that Amazon takes a larger percentage of ebook revenue than print revenue. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=1275" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:15</a> | Have you considered signed copies? | He has, but shipping from Canada makes them prohibitively expensive; signed book plates are the more feasible option. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=1566" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">26:06</a> | Did Moses's staff transform into a crocodile in Pharaoh's palace? | He said the text says a serpent-like creature, not clearly a crocodile, and personally thinks a Nile monitor is more plausible. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=1709" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">28:29</a> | Did Egyptians practice transformation magic like that? | He said Egyptian texts do preserve transformation stories, including wax-crocodile material. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=1857" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">30:57</a> | Did Samson get his strength from his hair, from the Lord, or both? | He said the power came from the Lord, with Samson's hair functioning as part of the Nazirite sign tied to that calling. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=2116" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">35:16</a> | How likely is the tradition that Thomas traveled to India to preach the gospel? | He said the tradition is more plausible than many assume because names once doubted in the account later received archaeological support. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=2265" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">37:45</a> | Was God's presence in the Second Temple? | Yes. He argued that the tearing of the veil at Christ's crucifixion indicates God's presence was there. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=2425" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">40:25</a> | Is the tearing of the temple veil related to tearing clothing? | Yes in the sense that the veil was cloth; he also noted that temple veils were not unique to Israelite temples. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=2486" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">41:26</a> | If you wrote an Exodus commentary, what would your funding goal be? | He said funding is no longer the main obstacle; publishing and marketing an independent commentary would be the harder problem. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=2774" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">46:14</a> | What is a parish? | He defined it as a district overseen by a church authority, and noted the term can also be used for political districts. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=2945" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">49:05</a> | What is something you wish people knew more about the crucifixion and Christ's passion? | He emphasized that nails really were used, including with wooden washers, and cited archaeological evidence from a crucified man's ankle bone. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=3121" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">52:01</a> | What does "It is finished" mean? | He said it points to a purpose completed and a debt paid, not merely that the ordeal was over. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=3249" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">54:09</a> | Does "It is finished" connect to the Thummim? | No. He said Thummim is a separate Hebrew term with its own uncertainties. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=3285" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">54:45</a> | If we saw Eve in the garden, what would Satan have looked like? | He said Satan would be an angelic or spiritual being, but human perception may shape whether he appeared serpentine or otherwise. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=3455" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">57:35</a> | What do you think of Dr. Siguad's model of the Trinity? | He said it is a valiant attempt but skirts modalism and does not successfully resolve the Trinitarian issue. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=3639" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:39</a> | Is modalism a heresy? | Yes. He said modalism is absolutely a heresy. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=3705" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:01:45</a> | What do you think of William Lane Craig's Cerberus analogy? | He said it is a weak analogy that risks moving toward partialism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=3793" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:03:13</a> | Is monarchial trinitarianism heresy or orthodoxy? | It can be orthodox if it means one God in three persons, but the terminology must be used carefully. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=3893" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:04:53</a> | Is only the Father God, or are the three persons the one God? | He said the three persons are the one God, with later church language reconciling Father, Son, and Spirit with divine unity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=4190" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:09:50</a> | Is the Trinity a logical necessity? | He said it helps answer how God can be good before creation, because goodness can be expressed within the Trinity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=4253" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:10:53</a> | Why is the Athanasian Creed not authoritative for Orthodox Christians? | He said Eastern Orthodox Christians do hold to it as part of tradition, though they do not recite it and interpret it through eternal generation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=4818" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:20:18</a> | Do Terah leaving Ur and Abraham fighting Hammurabi make sense within the biblical narrative? | He said the events are close in time, but not necessarily in the same order, and the date window may not be as straightforward as it appears. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=4920" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:22:00</a> | Is adoptionistic Christology anachronistic? | He said adoptionism is a heresy that leads toward low Christology and a kind of created lower deity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=5036" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:23:56</a> | Why is the Old Testament so underrated? | He said many readers approach it too simplistically, often through modern Marcionite assumptions about wrath and violence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=5264" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:27:44</a> | What does Deuteronomy 12:15 mean? | He said the passage allows ordinary slaughter and eating within one's gates, with clean and unclean referring to people rather than animals. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=5438" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:30:38</a> | How are "begotten of" and "proceeding from" different? | He said the terms overlap but differ in nuance: begotten implies relationship, while proceeding can involve being sent or a procession of substance. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=5638" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:33:58</a> | What are your thoughts on the filioque? | He said the Orthodox are right that the filioque is wrong, but for the wrong reasons; he grounds the issue in the Father sending the Spirit at the Son's behest. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=5826" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:37:06</a> | How do you water seed with your feet in Deuteronomy 11:10? | He said Egyptian irrigation used canals and small channels made with the feet to move water to crops. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=5988" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:39:48</a> | Were shovels considered unclean or just expensive? | He said metal tools were expensive, so ordinary farmers often used their feet rather than hoes or shovels. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=6131" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:42:11</a> | What does Mark 9:49 mean? | He said "salted with fire" draws on offering language and points to purification and works being tested by fire. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=6540" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:49:00</a> | If the Levites start carrying the ark after Aaron's death in Deuteronomy 10:6-8, who carried it before? | He said the clan of Kohath carried it earlier, and the Deuteronomy passage is likely a scribal gloss reflecting later Levitical identity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=6815" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:53:35</a> | What was the ancient Israelite perception of Sheol? | He said both righteous and unrighteous dead went to Sheol in the Old Testament, with later Second Temple thought developing a divided Sheol. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=7157" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:59:17</a> | With the state of seminaries today, what hope is there for proper theological education, especially Old Testament and history? | He said it is increasingly difficult to find quality theological education, though Dallas Theological Seminary's THM Old Testament department may still be a decent option. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=7578" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:06:18</a> | What is the best period of Doctor Who? | He prefers classic Doctor Who, especially the late 1960s through early 1980s, with the second through fourth Doctors as a high point. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=7727" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:08:47</a> | How do we know what is a scribal gloss in the Old Testament? | He said glosses tend to be parenthetical explanations and often introduce anachronisms. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=8035" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:13:55</a> | How do we know Zoan is Tanis? | He said Zoan is the Semitic name for Tanis. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=8231" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:17:11</a> | Could the writer have meant a rebuild of Hebron rather than the location itself? | He said he does not think so, because Hebron was not rebuilt in that way before Tanis. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=8388" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:19:48</a> | Does Genesis 34:23 refer to two tribes becoming one? | He said no; clan cultures do not merge that way, and the passage is more like one group trying to absorb another. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=8570" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:22:50</a> | Did the Benjaminites eat pork? | He said yes, they raised and ate pork, partly because they were separated from the rest of the Israelites by the Jordan. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=8730" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:25:30</a> | Did the Benjaminites ever stop eating pork? | He said he does not think they stopped, and that they were still eating pork into the Roman period. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=8962" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:29:22</a> | Did people know how to fry food back then? | Yes. He said frying was more an Iron Age practice because it required a vessel suitable for hot oil. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=9115" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:31:55</a> | What are some other double-offense judgments? | He gave David's adultery with Bathsheba and murder of Uriah as an example. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=9325" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:35:25</a> | Is there a book that views Deuteronomy alongside Exodus, Leviticus, and Numbers? | He said he does not know of one offhand, though the question is interesting. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=9542" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:39:02</a> | Does Deuteronomy 10:4 clarify whether God or Moses wrote on the second tablets? | He said the second-tablet text remains ambiguous, because God may have dictated while Moses wrote. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=9722" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:42:02</a> | Did ancient Israelites put blood on the doorposts every Passover, or was that a one-time thing? | He said it was a one-time thing for the first Passover, not a repeated annual ritual. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=10082" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:48:02</a> | Did everyone write today? | He said he did not write that day because he was working on video-game modeling and rendering in Blender. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=10207" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:50:07</a> | Based on John 14:1-3, did the destination of believers after death change after the resurrection? | Yes. He said believers moved from Sheol to heaven after the resurrection, but the final destination is the new heavens and new earth. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=10399" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:53:19</a> | What if the groom did not have the resources or knowledge to build a house? | He said then the groom could not get married, because providing a home was part of the marriage expectation. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=10790" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:59:50</a> | What do you think about the patriarchs and the fossils of camels? | He said camel domestication by the early second millennium BC is well established, so patriarchal camels should not be treated as a problem. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=11151" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:05:51</a> | In what areas of Jericho did Kathleen Kenyon see evidence of destruction? | He said Garstang found the Late Bronze IIB destruction-layer fragment in the middle building, while Kenyon found Late Bronze IIB pottery. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=11751" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:15:51</a> | Did the Egyptians believe pyramids or obelisks channeled energy as alchemists claim? | No. He said obelisks were solar monuments, while pyramids functioned more like royal resurrection machinery, not energy channels. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=11971" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:19:31</a> | Are you a Thomist? | No. He said he is not a Thomist and never has been. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=12037" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:20:37</a> | What is a Thomist? | He defined a Thomist as someone who follows the philosophy of Thomas Aquinas. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=12124" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:22:04</a> | Do only special Egyptians get tombs? | He said tombs depended on economic status: anyone who could afford one could have one, while the poor were usually buried in sand. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=12499" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:28:19</a> | Why do some churches completely disregard the Old Testament? | He said many pastors either do not understand it or treat it as obsolete, which he described as a kind of cultural Marcionism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=13178" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:39:38</a> | Who wrote the book titled Biblical Archaeology next to Garstang's Story of Jericho on your shelf? | He identified it as a 1974 volume edited by Shalom Paul and William Dever, but recommended newer introductions instead. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=13482" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:44:42</a> | What do you think about Samaritan Pentateuch textual variants versus the Masoretic Text? | He said the Masoretic Text, Septuagint, and Samaritan Pentateuch are all important textual traditions for Pentateuchal criticism. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=13604" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:46:44</a> | If the battle of kings was in Hammurabi's 39th year, would we know the king of Elam? | He said no, because Elamite king lists and reign lengths are too uncertain, with multiple possible candidates. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=13880" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:51:20</a> | Why is Kip involved in the Exodus and conquest debate if his expertise is in Dead Sea Scrolls? | He dismissed the involvement as ideologically driven and not worth engaging. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=14094" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:54:54</a> | What do you think of the claim that Yahweh's original nature was as a solar deity rather than a storm god? | He rejected both labels, saying Yahweh controls nature but is not reducible to a solar or storm deity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=14548" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:02:28</a> | Would Moses have used Ivri script or Proto-Sinaitic script? | He said Moses probably used an alphabetic script, likely some kind of Proto-Sinaitic or early alphabetic form, rather than Egyptian. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=14935" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:08:55</a> | Is there new evidence for a pre-Fourth Dynasty Sphinx? | He said minor sphinxes could potentially be earlier, but he strongly rejected claims that the main Great Sphinx predates the Fourth Dynasty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/Gjz-v5Wi804?t=15257" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:14:17</a> | Have we barely scratched the surface of ancient Egypt archaeology because of the Egyptian government? | He said the reasons include government gatekeeping, funding, institutional affiliation, and local control over promising sites. |
